title: Transport & Getting Around
description: Practice transport vocabulary, the difference between jít and jet, motion verbs with destination cases, and asking for directions in dialogues.

exercises.prompt: What does 'jít' mean and when do you use it?

data.question_cz: Co znamená 'jít' a kdy se používá?
data.question_en: What does 'jít' mean and when do you use it?
data.explanation: jít = to go on foot, to walk. Use jít when moving by walking. jet = to go by vehicle (car, bus, train, etc.).

exercises.prompt: Complete with jít or jet (conjugated): 'I go to school on foot.'

data.explanation: jít → jdu (I go/walk). pěšky = on foot. Use jít because walking is involved.

exercises.prompt: Complete with jít or jet (conjugated): 'He goes to Prague by train.'

data.explanation: jet → jede (he goes by vehicle). vlakem = by train (instrumental). do Prahy = to Prague (genitive after 'do').

exercises.prompt: Which is a form of transport in Czech?

data.question_en: Which word is a means of transport?
data.explanation: autobus = bus. Common transport: auto (car), vlak (train), tramvaj (tram), metro (subway), kolo (bicycle), letadlo (plane).
Kam = where to (direction/motion). jdeš = you go (on foot). 'Kde jsi?' = Where are you? (location, no motion). 'Kam jedeš?' = Where are you going? (by vehicle).

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'Where are you going?' (asking someone on foot)

data.grammar_note: Kam = where to (destination). jdeš = you go (walking). Contrast with 'Kde jsi?' = Where are you? (location).

exercises.prompt: Complete: motion (do/na) vs location (v/na). Fill in the correct preposition.

data.sentence: Jdu ___ školy. (to school — motion) × Jsem ___ škole. (at school — location)
data.explanation: do + genitive = to (motion toward). v/ve + locative = in/at (location). 'Jdu do školy.' = I'm going to school. 'Jsem ve škole.' = I'm at school.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Jedeme ___ vlakem.' (We go by train — motion)

data.question_en: Choose the correct form: 'We go ___ train.'
data.explanation: Wait — actually 'jet vlakem' uses the instrumental case without a preposition for the means of transport. But if you're saying 'We go by train to Prague': 'Jedeme vlakem do Prahy.' The prepositions do/na are for the destination, not the transport. This question checks: v = in (locative) for location, do/na = to (motion). With transport, use instrumental (vlakem, autem, autobusem).

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I'm going to the cinema.' (motion, on foot)

data.grammar_note: do + genitive for enclosed places: do kina (kino → kina in genitive). jdu = I go (on foot). Motion uses do/na + genitive/accusative.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'Excuse me, where is the tram stop?'

data.explanation: 'Promiňte, kde je tramvajová zastávka?' = 'Excuse me, where is the tram stop?' promiňte = excuse me (formal), tramvajová zastávka = tram stop.

exercises.prompt: Read the dialogue asking for directions. What does person B answer to 'Jak se dostanu na nádraží?'

lines.speaker: person_a
lines.text: Promiňte, jak se dostanu na nádraží?

lines.speaker: person_b
lines.text: Jděte rovně a pak doprava. Nádraží je naproti parku.

lines.speaker: person_b
lines.text: Ne, je to blízko. Asi pět minut pěšky.

accepted_answers: Jděte rovně a pak doprava. Nádraží je naproti parku.|Rovně a doprava.|Jděte rovně, pak doprava.
data.explanation: 'Jak se dostanu na nádraží?' = 'How do I get to the station?' The response gives directions: go straight, then right. 'Asi pět minut pěšky' = about five minutes on foot.

exercises.answer_key: Jděte rovně a pak doprava. Nádraží je naproti parku.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear (asking for directions).

data.note: 'Jak se dostanu na letiště?' = 'How do I get to the airport?' letiště = airport (neuter). dostanu se = I get (reflexive verb dostat se).
